payload.title: MTA Board Action Items
payload.content_type: board meeting minutes and action items
payload.description: MTA Board Meeting materials including minutes from May 28, 2025 meeting, procurement actions, real estate items, advertising policy updates, and capital plan items for the June 25, 2025 Board meeting.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge Main Cable Dehumidification
tool_args.raw_text: Design-Build Services for the Main Cable Dehumidification at the Verrazzano Narrows Bridge. Skanska Koch Inc., Contract No. VN-8Q, $249,003,000, 1,521 Calendar Days

payload.description: Installation of a cable dehumidification system on the four main cables of the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge to prevent corrosion and preserve cable strength, including acoustic monitoring, electrical systems, cable inspection, and maintenance for five years after commissioning.

warnings: project.implementing_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Implementing agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: M-9A Passenger Railcars Procurement
tool_args.raw_text: Contract award for M-9A Passenger Railcars Procurement. Alstom Transportation Inc., $2,304,271,712 (estimated), 316 base order cars (160 LIRR/156 MNR), Base: September 2036 / Option: March 2038

payload.description: Design, manufacture, test, furnish, and delivery of 316 self-powered base order passenger railcars (160 for LIRR, 156 for MNR) to replace MNR M-3 cars and retire LIRR M-3 cars supporting Grand Central Madison service expansion. Includes options for 100-242 additional cars.

warnings: project.implementing_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Implementing agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: LIRR Audio Visual Recording Monitoring System Upgrade
tool_args.raw_text: Gatekeeper System USA Inc., Contract # 400000000003442, $22,951,498 (estimated), Three years + Warranty Period. Contract award for replacement of LIRR's current AVRM system on its M7 fleets, C3 coaches, Diesel-Electric Dual Mode 30, and adding the system to the M3 fleet, totaling 1,087 railcars.

payload.project_name: LIRR Audio Visual Recording Monitoring System Upgrade
payload.description: Replacement of LIRR's current Audio-Visual Recording Monitoring system on M7 fleets, C3 coaches, Diesel-Electric Dual Mode 30, and adding system to M3 fleet (1,087 total railcars) to comply with FRA mandate requiring CCTV with forward-facing and cab cameras by October 12, 2027.

warnings: project.implementing_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Implementing agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: NYCT Bus CMMS to Hexagon Enterprise Asset Management System
tool_args.raw_text: 21Tech LLC, Contract # 900000000005018, $13,001,280, Up to 8 years (4-year base + 2 two-year options). Consulting services to replace a 29-year-old Computer Maintenance Management System in 27 bus depots and four shops within the five boroughs to Hexagon EAM.

payload.project_name: NYCT Bus CMMS to Hexagon Enterprise Asset Management System
payload.description: Replace legacy 29-year-old Computer Maintenance Management System in 27 bus depots and four shops within the five boroughs to a new Hexagon Enterprise Asset Management System for NYC Transit's Department of Buses and MTA Bus Company.

warnings: project.implementing_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Implementing agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: FMTAC Investment Portfolio Manager Contract
tool_args.raw_text: BlackRock Financial Management Inc., $2,848,750, Five-years, Contract # 15900. Portfolio manager for FMTAC, MTA's wholly owned captive insurance company.

payload.description: Award of competitively negotiated personal service contract to BlackRock Financial Management Inc. as Portfolio Manager for assets held within First Mutual Transportation Assurance Company (FMTAC). Initial term of 3 years with two 1-year options.

warnings: project.implementing_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Implementing agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: MTA-wide Energy Management System
tool_args.raw_text: Tango Analytics LLC, $2,313,338 (estimate), up to five years (3-year base + two 1-year options), Contract # 90000000004944. MTA-wide Energy Management System for utility billing data management.

payload.description: Furnish and implement an MTA-wide Energy Management System for utility billing data management and efficient management of MTA energy use through data collection, reporting monitoring and user engagement. Replaces current EMS provided by SourceOne.

warnings: project.implementing_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Implementing agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: Queens Boulevard West Line CBTC Engineering Support (SYSTRA Modification)
tool_args.raw_text: SYSTRA Engineering, Inc., Contract CM1539, $2,094,000. Ratification of modification for additional engineering support services for installation of CBTC on NYCT's Queens Boulevard West Line.

payload.project_name: Queens Boulevard West Line CBTC Engineering Support
payload.description: Ratification of a modification to contract CM-1539 for additional engineering support services in support of the installation of a Communication Based Train Control signaling system on NYCT's Queens Boulevard West Line, covering services from April 30, 2023 to February 26, 2024.

warnings: project.implementing_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Implementing agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: 42nd Street Corridor Projects - Naik Consulting Modification
tool_args.raw_text: Naik Consulting Group, PC, Contract PS886, $8,564,600. Ratification of modification for continued project management services for three 42nd Street Corridor projects.

payload.project_name: 42nd Street Corridor Projects - Program Management Extension
payload.description: Ratification of modification to extend contract by 19 months through April 30, 2026 for continued project management services for three ongoing 42nd Street Corridor projects: escalator replacement at Grand Central (Flushing Line), circulation improvements at Grand Central-42nd Street Station, and new elevators at Bryant Park-42nd Street and Fifth Avenue Station.

warnings: project.implementing_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Implementing agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: R179 CBTC Carborne Software Modification - 8th Avenue Line
tool_args.raw_text: Siemens Mobility, Inc., Contract S-48013-1, $3,200,000. Modification to provide for changes to the carbone controller software on R179 subway cars to improve system performance and reliability for the 8th Avenue Line CBTC.

payload.project_name: R179 CBTC Carborne Software Modification - 8th Avenue Line
payload.description: Modification to contract S-48013-1 for changes to carbone controller software on R179 subway cars to improve performance and reliability, including better alignment with short platforms, improved functioning in work zones, and smoother yard-to-mainline transitions.

warnings: project.implementing_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Implementing agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: R211 CBTC Carborne Software Modification - 8th Avenue Line (Hitachi)
tool_args.raw_text: Hitachi Rail GTS USA Inc., Contract S-48013-2, $1,350,000. Modification to provide for multiple changes to the carbone controller software on R211 subway cars to improve system performance and reliability for the 8th Avenue Line CBTC.

payload.project_name: R211 CBTC Carborne Software Modification - 8th Avenue Line
payload.description: Modification to contract S-48013-2 for multiple changes to carbone controller software on R211 subway cars including: disabling ATO upon loss of ATS communication, providing date/time from ATS, updating track info from wayside, and faster radio switchover.

warnings: project.implementing_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Implementing agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: R251 Vacuum Train 4 Fire Damage Repair (NEU International Railways)
tool_args.raw_text: NEU International Railways, $1,658,620 (not-to-exceed), March 29, 2024-March 31, 2026. Emergency repair services on R251 Vacuum Train 4 (VT4); Ratification of an Immediate Operating Need. Fire damage repair of Filter Car 2.

payload.project_name: R251 Vacuum Train 4 Fire Damage Repair
payload.description: Emergency repair service contract on R251 Vacuum Train 4 to replace fire-damaged Filter Car 2 interior workings. Original Equipment Manufacturer NEU International Railways will fabricate and install new filter car module, wiring, and operating software.

warnings: project.implementing_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Implementing agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: Paratransit Technology System Transition - Marine Tiger Consulting
tool_args.raw_text: Marine Tiger Technologies Corporation, $5.6 million, three-year noncompetitive personal service contract. Project management consulting services for transition to new Paratransit Technology System.

payload.description: Three-year noncompetitive personal service contract to Marine Tiger Technologies Corporation for project management consulting services for the transition to the new Paratransit Technology System which provides scheduling and routing support for daily Paratransit operations.

warnings: project.implementing_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Implementing agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: May 2025 MTA Board and Finance Committee Meeting
tool_args.raw_text: May 28, 2025 - Noon - 2 Broadway, New York, NY - Joint Minutes of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority, the New York City Transit Authority... May 2025 Finance Committee and Regular Board Meeting Minutes

payload.description: May 2025 Finance Committee and Regular Board Meeting. Meeting called to order by Chair Janno Lieber, included public comment, chair's remarks, capital plan funding discussion, capital plan resubmission approval, and executive session on labor matters.

tool_args.label: 150+ Accessible Stations Today
tool_args.raw_text: Today we have well more than 150 accessible stations, additional 36 projects in construction, 25 more in procurement or design, and 60+ projects included in our new Capital Program.

payload.description: Number of accessible MTA stations as of June 2025

tool_args.label: Elevator Availability 98.4%
tool_args.raw_text: As of last month, elevator availability is at 98.4%, which is five points higher than pre-COVID and well above our agency goal of 96.5%.

payload.description: MTA elevator availability rate as of May 2025

tool_args.label: Elevator Availability Jan 2020: 93.4%
tool_args.raw_text: In January 2020, right before COVID, we had 115 accessible stations, and the availability rate was 93.4%.

payload.description: MTA elevator availability rate in January 2020, pre-COVID

tool_args.label: Fare Revenue at 78% of Pre-COVID Levels
tool_args.raw_text: fare revenue is surging. We are at 78% of pre-COVID levels thanks partly to our effective efforts to push back against fare evasion.

payload.description: Fare revenue as percentage of pre-COVID levels

tool_args.label: Subway Fare Evasion Reduced 30%
tool_args.raw_text: Three years later, looking at last summer to now, subway fare evasion has been reduced by 30%.

payload.description: Subway fare evasion reduction from last summer to current

tool_args.label: 36% Fare Evasion Reduction in Stations with Interventions
tool_args.raw_text: Together those different investments and deployments have contributed to a 36% reduction in those stations that have all of the different interventions.

payload.description: Fare evasion reduction in stations with combined interventions (gate guards, delayed egress, turnstile fins/sleeves)

tool_args.label: MTA Portfolio Net Earnings $509M in 2024
tool_args.raw_text: In 2024, MTA-managed portfolio achieved the net earnings of $509 million and the weighted average yield of 5.05%.

payload.description: MTA-managed portfolio net earnings for 2024

tool_args.label: Weighted Average Yield 5.05%
tool_args.raw_text: MTA-managed portfolio achieved the net earnings of $509 million and the weighted average yield of 5.05%.

payload.description: MTA-managed portfolio weighted average yield for 2024

tool_args.label: MTA Portfolio Size $10 Billion
tool_args.raw_text: the MTA-managed $10 billion portfolio is invested in short Treasury securities

payload.description: MTA-managed portfolio size

tool_args.label: Congestion Relief Revenue April 2025: $56.7M
tool_args.raw_text: Congestion relief revenue for the month of April 2025 continues to be on target with projections, as the program collected $56.7 million. The total Year-to-date revenue for the program is $215.7 million.

payload.description: Congestion relief revenue collected in April 2025

tool_args.label: Congestion Relief Revenue YTD: $215.7M
tool_args.raw_text: The total Year-to-date revenue for the program is $215.7 million.

payload.description: Year-to-date congestion relief revenue through April 2025

tool_args.label: Congestion Relief Net Revenue First 4 Months: $169M
tool_args.raw_text: Accrued expenses totaled $46.7 million as of April 2025. The net revenue for the first four months of the congestion relief program is $169 million.

payload.description: Net revenue from congestion relief program for first four months (revenue minus accrued expenses)

tool_args.label: MTA 2025-2029 Capital Plan Resubmission Approved
tool_args.raw_text: Resubmission of the MTA 2025-2029 Capital Plan to the Capital Plan Review Board.

payload.claim_text: The Board approved the Resubmission of the MTA 2025-2029 Capital Plan to the Capital Plan Review Board.
payload.subject: MTA 2025-2029 Capital Plan
payload.description: Board approved resubmission of the 2025-2029 Capital Plan to the Capital Plan Review Board at the May 28, 2025 meeting.

tool_args.label: Queens Transfer Fare Policy Promotional Launch
tool_args.raw_text: requested board approval to launch a promotional transfer policy, beginning on June 29, 2025, when Phase One of the Queens bus network redesign takes effect. The promotional policy would last for approximately six months, until January 2026.

payload.claim_text: Board approved launch of a promotional Queens Transfer Fare Policy beginning June 29, 2025, when Phase One of the Queens Bus Network Redesign takes effect, lasting approximately six months until January 2026.
payload.subject: Queens Transfer Fare Policy
payload.description: Promotional transfer policy approved for Queens bus network redesign Phase One launch

tool_args.label: MTA Advertising Policy Updated
tool_args.raw_text: Board approval of updates to the MTA Advertising Policy... updated policy clarifies the scope... clarifies requirements for Large Format Advertising... maintains alcohol advertising in Prohibited category while allowing in limited settings

payload.claim_text: Board approved updates to the MTA Advertising Policy including clarifications on scope, Large Format Advertising requirements, and limited allowances for alcohol advertising on digital displays in stations during non-student commutation hours, on S shuttle train wraps, in major commuter rail hubs, and on billboards with 500-foot restrictions.
payload.subject: MTA Advertising Policy
payload.description: MTA Advertising Policy updated with new provisions for alcohol advertising and Large Format Advertising

tool_args.label: Capital Program Approved by Legislature
tool_args.raw_text: the whole Capital Program got approved by the Legislature this month. It's the largest program in MTA history and it is the first capital program to be based on a comprehensive review of the condition of this priceless system

payload.claim_text: The MTA Capital Program was approved by the Legislature this month (June 2025). It is the largest program in MTA history and the first capital program based on a comprehensive review of system condition, prioritizing State of Good Repair.
payload.subject: MTA Capital Program
payload.description: MTA Capital Program approved by State Legislature - largest in MTA history, prioritizing State of Good Repair

tool_args.label: All-Agency Military Benefits Policy Approved
tool_args.raw_text: Board approval of a new All Agency Military Benefits Policy, as required by Public Authorities Law Section 2824. Provides all-agency guidance on pay and leave benefits for qualifying service members during military leave.

payload.claim_text: Board approved the new All-Agency Military Benefits Policy addressing pay and leave benefits for qualifying service members during military leave, as required by Public Authorities Law Section 2824.
payload.subject: All-Agency Military Benefits Policy
payload.description: New MTA All-Agency Military Benefits Policy approved by Board

tool_args.label: First Mutual Transportation Assurance Company Annual Meeting 2025
tool_args.raw_text: Jaibala Patel, Co-Chief Financial Officer, presented annual highlights of the captive insurance program and two action items for board approval - Cybersecurity Certification and Business Continuity Plan/Disaster Recovery Plan updates.

payload.description: Annual meeting of First Mutual Transportation Assurance Company (FMTAC), the MTA's captive insurance company. Board approved Cybersecurity Certification of Material Compliance and updated Business Continuity Plan and Disaster Recovery Plan.
